skill.name: space-cooking-adventure
skill.description: Guides an interactive, light-hearted sci-fi cooking chapter where Stella experiments with alien ingredients on Gaia.
skill.summary: Create a humorous space survival cooking scene with staged attempts, failures, and warm character moments.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external commands, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and entropy concerns, but review found these are false positives in a readable Markdown story prompt. The flagged backticks are fenced narrative examples, and no evidence found of executable code, network access, filesystem access, secret handling, or prompt injection that attempts to bypass review.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The detected backtick locations are Markdown code fences that contain staged dialogue and scene directions. They do not invoke Ruby, shell commands, subprocesses, or user-controlled execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding lines show plain Markdown fences enclosing narrative text. There are no executable command strings, interpreters, or argument construction patterns.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are metadata and narrative text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, cipher selection, hashing APIs, or cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of both reported lines shows story description or dialogue only. The skill contains no code that performs cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance location is part of a character dialogue about sharing moments. No evidence found of host discovery, environment probing, identity checks, or system commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural-language story text, not a command or API call. Adjacent context is a closing emotional scene, not system inspection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: The file is a readable Markdown prompt with mixed Chinese and English prose. No evidence found of binary payloads, encoded blobs, encrypted content, or hidden instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The whole file renders as normal front matter and story guidance. The entropy score is plausibly caused by multilingual prose and punctuation rather than obfuscation.

content.value_statement: Writers need playful filler scenes that still build character and momentum. This skill provides a structured space cooking chapter with humor, staged recipe attempts, and emotional payoff.
actual_capabilities: Defines Stella Chen as the viewpoint character for a light sci-fi cooking episode.
Provides a five-stage interaction flow from inspiration through a warm closing scene.
Supplies sample dialogue beats for failed and successful alien ingredient recipes.
Lists alternate endings for chef success, repeated disasters, and cultural exchange.
Gives writing guidance for comedy timing, pacing, realism, and emotional depth.
limitations: It is a narrative prompt, not an executable tool or game engine.
It is tailored to the Stella, Gaia, and Eos story setting.
It does not provide real food safety or survival cooking advice.
It includes bilingual Chinese and English prose, which may need localization.

use_cases.title: Add Comic Relief to a Sci-Fi Campaign
use_cases.description: Use the staged cooking attempts to create a lighter chapter between intense survival events.

use_cases.title: Develop Stella Through Everyday Choices
use_cases.description: Show Stella as curious, imperfect, and resilient while she experiments with alien ingredients.

use_cases.title: Prototype Branching Story Outcomes
use_cases.description: Use the provided endings to test success, failure, and cultural exchange paths for a narrative scene.

prompt_templates.title: Start the Cooking Scene
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a light roleplay chapter.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the space-cooking-adventure skill to begin Stella Chen cooking with Gaia ingredients. Keep the tone playful and focused on her first attempt.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Failed Recipe Beat
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate scene expansion with physical comedy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write the next interaction where Stella tries one alien ingredient combination and it goes wrong in a funny but harmless way.

prompt_templates.title: Balance Humor With Character Growth
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced chapter pacing and emotional tone.
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue the chapter with one successful recipe, then connect the food to Stella missing home and adapting to Gaia.

prompt_templates.title: Design Branching Endings
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced narrative design for replayable outcomes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three possible endings for the cooking chapter: a chef success, a disaster recovery, and a cultural exchange with Eos.

output_examples.input: Begin Stella Chen trying to improve emergency rations with alien fruit.
output_examples.output: A playful scene where Stella complains about ration bars, gathers strange fruit, and asks the player to witness a likely disaster.

output_examples.input: Give me a successful recipe moment after several failures.
output_examples.output: Stella creates a Gaia burger using safe fruit juice, edible leaves, ration powder, and a small amount of Earth spice.

output_examples.input: End the chapter with a warm emotional beat.
output_examples.output: Stella reflects that survival includes joy, shared meals, and small comforts that make Gaia feel less lonely.

best_practices: Keep each recipe attempt short, visual, and easy for the player to react to.
Use failures for humor while keeping Stella capable and emotionally believable.
End with a concrete recipe, lesson, or relationship beat that changes the chapter.
anti_patterns: Do not turn the chapter into real survival cooking instructions.
Do not make Stella instantly expert at cooking without trial and error.
Do not let jokes erase the warmth, loneliness, or character development in the scene.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create a humorous sci-fi cooking chapter for Stella Chen on Gaia.

faq.question: Is this a real cooking guide?
faq.answer: No. It is a fictional writing and roleplay guide for alien food scenes.

faq.question: Can it support branching outcomes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes success, failure, and cultural exchange ending ideas.

faq.question: Does it require any external tools?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown prompt and does not call scripts or services.

faq.question: Who is the main character?
faq.answer: The skill is written for Stella Chen, a survivor experimenting with Gaia ingredients.

faq.question: Can I adapt it to another story?
faq.answer: Yes. You can replace Stella, Gaia, ingredients, and story references with your own setting.
